##NAMES=Steenstrupine-(Ce)
##RRUFFID=R100205
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Na_14_Ce_6_Mn^2+^_2_Fe^3+^_2_Zr(PO_4_)_7_Si_12_O_36_(OH)_2_·3H_2_O
##LOCALITY=Mont Saint-Hilaire, Rouville County, Quebec, Canada
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Royal Ontario Museum
##DESCRIPTION=Transparent brown blocky crystals on a white sodalite matrix. Our sample is twinned. If we ignored the twin, then we get the reported hexagonal cell of Moore and Shen (1983).
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral has been confirmed only by single crystal X-ray diffraction
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R100205
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated (hexagonal cell a=10.4602(8), c=45.457(5))
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 18.13 b: 10.4602 c: 19.375 alpha: 90 beta: 128.537 gamma: 90 volume: 2874.1 crystal system: monoclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=780
